title: Engineering Mathematics - Linear Algebra - Cartesian Coordinates

questions.question:
Let \( P_{1},P_{2},\ldots,P_{n} \) be \( n \) points in the \( xy- \)plane such that no three of them are collinear. For every pair of points \( P_{i} \) and \( P_{j} \), let \( L_{ij} \) be the line passing through them. Let \( L_{ab} \) be the line with the steepest gradient amongst all \( \frac{n(n -1)}{2} \) lines.

Which one of the following properties should necessarily be satisfied ?



GATE IT 2007 | Question: 80
options: \( P_{a} \) and \( P_{b} \) are adjacent to each other with respect to their \( x \)-coordinate
Either \( P_{a} \) or \( P_{b} \) has the largest or the smallest \( y \)-coordinate among all the points
The difference between \( x \)-coordinates \( P_{a} \) and \( P_{b} \) is minimum
questions.correct_answer: \( P_{a} \) and \( P_{b} \) are adjacent to each other with respect to their \( x \)-coordinate
